rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a contribution claim (38% p99 reduction, queue-aware admission policy) and an implicit gap statement (naive load balancers are cost-unaware); reviewing requires assessing whether the thesis is coherently stated and whether the gap framing is accurate.
rationale.R1.2: Logical flow spans multiple paragraphs with interleaved architecture description and motivation; internal consistency must be checked — e.g., the claim that queue-depth estimation avoids polling RTT overhead needs to follow from the described mechanism, and the two-level scheduler description must cohere across paragraphs.
rationale.R1.3: Two quantitative claims are made in-section (38% lower p99, MAE of 41 tokens) and attributed to external sections (§4 and §3.2); a review must flag whether those cross-references are appropriate anchors for the claims and whether anything asserted in this section outruns what the cited evidence can support.
rationale.R1.6: The text has identifiable prose defects: paragraph 2 is a run-on sentence with no principal break; paragraph 4 contains a tense error ('admission controller compared' should be 'compares'); paragraph 5 contains a comma splice joining two independent clauses with a comma rather than a conjunction or semicolon. Writing polish is a standard output of any section review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize writing quality and prose clarity (grammar, sentence structure, flow), or technical and scientific correctness (claim validity, logical consistency, evidence-to-claim alignment) — or both equally?
